title: Generalize the check harness to gate every module, and match parts on heading lines
context: FACT: adding M2 exposed that O4/O10 were hardcoded to M1. FACT: O4 grepped part words anywhere in prose, so a stray phrase could fake a pass (flaky-risk flagged at end of run 1). DECISION from D001: each new module is a known, gated motion.
options_considered: Duplicate the M1 O4/O10 block per module (copy-paste, drifts)
Generalize O4/O10 to iterate modules/*/ and tighten O4 to heading-line matching
decision: Generalize: O4 iterates every modules/*/ requiring 5 persona files each with the 7 part HEADINGS (matched on '^#+ .*<part>', not anywhere in prose); O10 runs --selftest for every modules/*/exercise/*.py that supports it. Adding a module now auto-extends the gate with no harness edit.
consequences: The referee can't be fooled by prose, and scales for free to M3..M5. Slightly stricter: module authors must use real Markdown headings for the 7 parts (documented in AGENTS.md).
